meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Students: Solve Deployment Issues with Antigravity Template
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS app as a student developer.
content.answer_block: The 'can't deploy' bottleneck usually stems from complex environment configurations and fragile infrastructure setups. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you standardize your deployment pipeline, while Claude Code automates the configuration. This combination abstracts the backend complexity, allowing you to focus on building features rather than wrestling with servers.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic, your UI looks crisp, and the features are polished. Yet, the moment you attempt to push your code to production, you hit a wall of cryptic error logs, dependency conflicts, and server timeouts. The transition from local development to a live URL feels like crossing a chasm, leaving many promising student projects abandoned in a 'local-only' purgatory.

But the gap between a student project and a live SaaS isn't technical brilliance; it's a standardized workflow. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you aren't just deploying an app—you are installing an industry-grade foundation that automates the heavy lifting. Combined with Claude Code, you can bridge the gap from local success to global accessibility in a single afternoon.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Start by cloning the Antigravity template to ensure a clean, production-ready directory structure that handles environment variables and build scripts by default.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard template with support for Node.js, TypeScript, and automated deployment hooks.

steps.title: Configure Environment Parity
steps.description: Ensure your local environment matches your production environment to prevent 'it works on my machine' syndrome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .env.example file and map all required environment variables for a standard production deployment.

steps.title: Automate Dependency Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your dependencies and ensure they are compatible with the Antigravity deployment target.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current package.json and ensure all dependencies are compatible with the target serverless deployment environment.

steps.title: Execute Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Trigger the automated build and deployment process defined within the Antigravity template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the deployment pipeline and provide a status report on the build health.

steps.title: Verify Production Health
steps.description: Perform a final check to ensure the application is reachable and that all database connections are secure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Execute a health check on the production URL and log any latency or connectivity issues detected.

pro_tips: Always set your build output directory to a static folder to prevent cache invalidation issues on subsequent deployments.
Use 'Environment Scoping' to separate your development database from production data, even if you are using a free tier provider.
Leverage Claude Code's 'review' flag before every deployment to catch hardcoded API keys that might have slipped into your commits.
Implement a 'rollback' script in your package.json to revert to the last stable deployment hash instantly.
Monitor your deployment logs for 'cold start' times, especially if you are using serverless functions, and optimize your bundle size accordingly.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, focus on setting up CI/CD workflows to automate every future push. Move from manual deployments to 'push-to-deploy' to keep your velocity high as you build your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to turn your local code into a live, scalable product. Don't let deployment anxiety stop you from shipping your next big idea. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
